Recent papers

Recent papers on Legendre's conjecture, surfaced by the prominence of their authors in this directory.

Showing 15 papers, ranked by the Top 100 standing of their authors then by recency. Authors who appear in the Top 100 are shown in bold.

#TitleAuthorsPublishedBest rank
1Higher uniformity of arithmetic functions in short intervals II. Almost all intervalsKaisa Matomäki, Maksym Radziwiłł, Xuancheng Shao, Terence Tao, Joni Teräväinen2024-11#1
2On the frequency of small gaps between the primesAkos Magyar, Janos Pintz2025-05#2
3Large sieve inequality for sums of Legendre symbols over short intervalsIgor Shparlinski, Yixiu Xiao2026-04#4
4On the correlations between character sums of division polynomials under shiftsSubham Bhakta, Igor E. Shparlinski2025-07#4
5On the number of exceptional intervals to the prime number theorem in short intervalsAyla Gafni, Terence Tao2025-05#11
6On the largest prime factor of integers in short intervals IIIRunbo Li2026-01#12
7Primes in almost all short intervalsRunbo Li2024-07#12
8Diophantine approximation with primes from short intervalsStephan Baier, Sayantan Roy2025-12#32
9Small solutions of generic ternary quadratic congruences to general moduliStephan Baier, Aishik Chattopadhyay2024-08#32
10On Sidon sets with squares, cubes and quartics in short intervalsM. Z. Garaev, F. M. Garayev, S. V. Konyagin2026-02#37
11On Averages of Shifted Convolutions with Applications to $GL(2)$ and $GL(3)$ Fourier CoefficientsJiseong Kim2025-09#48
12Goldbach's Problem in short intervals for numbers with a missing digitJiseong Kim2024-12#48
13Sierpinski's Hypothesis H1Matt Visser2025-12#55
14Effective short intervals containing primesMatt Visser2025-08#55
15The n-th prime exponentiallyMatt Visser2025-04#55

Most cited (all time)

The ten most-cited Mathematics works on Legendre's conjecture, by citation count in OpenAlex. Results are filtered to the Mathematics field (OpenAlex field 26) and ranked by total citations.

The Top 100 ranking is designed to capture prominent, currently active researchers. If an author in this table is not linked to a profile, it is usually because they are deceased or less active than the researchers in the ranking, not because they are less significant to the field.

#TitleAuthorsYearCitations
1Prime Numbers in Short Intervals and a Generalized Vaughan Identity DOID. R. Heath‐Brown1982198
2On the distribution of primes in short intervals DOIP. X. Gallagher1976173
3Primes in short intervals. DOIHelmut Maier1985152
4Pair Correlation of Zeros and Primes in Short Intervals DOID. A. Goldston; Hugh L. Montgomery1987106
5Almost-primes in arithmetic progressions and short intervals DOID. R. Heath‐Brown197884
6Primes in short intervals DOIHenryk Iwaniec; J. Pintz198478
7Very Large Gaps between Consecutive Primes DOIJ. Pintz199770
8The number of primes in a short interval.D. R. Heath‐Brown198868
9Unusually large gaps between consecutive primes DOIHelmut Maier; Carl Pomerance199068
10The Variance of the Number of Prime Polynomials in Short Intervals and in Residue Classes DOIJonathan P. Keating; Zeév Rudnick201265
